panels.title: Data as of
panels.description: Newest synced sample across last night's sleep, HRV, breathing rate, and skin temperature. If this is more than a few hours old, the poller hasn't caught up yet — the panels below may still show the prior night.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT GREATEST(
  (SELECT max(end_time) FROM health.sleep_session),
  (SELECT max(sleep_end) FROM health.breathing_rate),
  (SELECT max(sleep_start) FROM health.nightly_temperature)
) AS "latest sync"

panels.title: Last night's sleep
panels.description: Heart rate over last night's main sleep session (± 10min padding), colored per-point by sleep stage. X axis is hours since sleep onset (see the "Sleep timing" panel below for the actual clock time this references) rather than wall-clock time, so this panel is locked to the actual sleep episode and can't drift depending on when during the day it's viewed (design.md D15). Stage coloring is numeric (stage_code 0-3) with a thresholds override, not a text field directly - xychart's color matching only ever considers numeric fields (confirmed in the plugin's own source), so a text stage column is silently ignored no matter how it's referenced.

targets.rawSql:
WITH ps AS (SELECT start_time, end_time FROM health.primary_sleep_session ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1),
hr AS (
  SELECT time_bucket('2 minutes', h.time) AS t, avg(h.bpm) AS bpm
  FROM health.heart_rate h, ps
  WHERE h.time >= ps.start_time - interval '10 minutes' AND h.time < ps.end_time + interval '10 minutes'
  GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT
  EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (hr.t - ps.start_time)) / 3600.0 AS hours_since_onset,
  hr.bpm,
  COALESCE(
    (SELECT CASE stg.level WHEN 'deep' THEN 0 WHEN 'light' THEN 1 WHEN 'rem' THEN 2 WHEN 'wake' THEN 3 ELSE 3 END
     FROM health.sleep_stage stg
     WHERE stg.time <= hr.t AND stg.time + (stg.seconds || ' seconds')::interval > hr.t AND NOT stg.is_short
     ORDER BY stg.time DESC LIMIT 1),
    3
  ) AS stage_code
FROM hr, ps
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Sleep timing
panels.description: The actual clock-time reference for the hour-offset X axes on the sleep/HR panels: hour 0 on "Last night's sleep" and "Yesterday's heart rate" is this bedtime; hour 0 on "Today's heart rate" is this wake time.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT start_time AS "bedtime", end_time AS "wake time"
FROM health.primary_sleep_session ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1

panels.title: Sleep stage colors
panels.description: xychart's built-in legend is series-level (would just show "bpm"), not per-point-category - it can't explain what each color on the hypnogram trace means, so this is a plain text swatch key instead.

panels.title: Readiness
panels.description: Composite of last night's HRV, resting HR, and sleep minutes vs their 30d baselines (sign-corrected, IQR-scaled). Vibe = average across available components. Caution = worst single component, so one bad metric can't be averaged away by otherwise-good ones. Not Fitbit's Sleep Score — that's Takeout-only and unavailable for API-synced nights (see design.md D11).

targets.rawSql:
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT day FROM health.primary_sleep_session ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1
),
components AS (
  SELECT metric,
         CASE metric WHEN 'resting_hr' THEN -1 ELSE 1 END
           * (value - median) / NULLIF(p75 - p25, 0) AS z
  FROM health.daily_baseline, latest
  WHERE daily_baseline.day = latest.day
    AND metric IN ('hrv_rmssd','resting_hr','sleep_minutes')
)
SELECT round(avg(z)::numeric, 2) AS "vibe", round(min(z)::numeric, 2) AS "caution"
FROM components

panels.title: Sleep duration
panels.description: Efficiency dropped from this card: it's Takeout-only (map_sleep leaves it unset for API-synced sessions, same class of gap as main_sleep/log_id), so it would read blank on every sync-only night. A naive minutes_asleep/time_in_bed approximation was tried and rejected: it undershoots Fitbit's real figure by a consistent 5-6 points on backfilled nights where both are known, which would be actively misleading, not just approximate.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT round(minutes_asleep / 60.0, 1) AS "hours asleep"
FROM health.primary_sleep_session ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1

panels.title: Overnight SpO2
panels.description: Average and minimum blood oxygen over last night's sleep window. Raw query, not baseline-compared — folding SpO2 into daily_baseline was tried and reverted for a performance regression (see design.md D14).

targets.rawSql:
WITH ps AS (SELECT start_time, end_time FROM health.primary_sleep_session ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1)
SELECT avg(sp.pct) AS "avg spo2 %", min(sp.pct) AS "min spo2 %"
FROM health.spo2 sp, ps
WHERE sp.time >= ps.start_time AND sp.time < ps.end_time

panels.title: Nap
panels.description: Any sleep_session on the same civil day as last night's primary session, other than the primary session itself.

targets.rawSql:
WITH latest_day AS (SELECT day FROM health.primary_sleep_session ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1)
SELECT round(ss.minutes_asleep::numeric, 0) AS "nap minutes"
FROM health.sleep_session ss, latest_day
WHERE ss.day = latest_day.day
  AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM health.primary_sleep_session ps WHERE ps.start_time = ss.start_time)
ORDER BY ss.start_time DESC LIMIT 1

SELECT value AS "rmssd (ms)", value - median AS "vs 30d median"
FROM health.daily_baseline WHERE metric = 'hrv_rmssd' ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1

panels.title: Resting HR vs 30d baseline
panels.description: Lower than baseline generally reads as a good recovery sign.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT value AS "resting bpm", value - median AS "vs 30d median"
FROM health.daily_baseline WHERE metric = 'resting_hr' ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1

panels.title: Breathing rate vs 30d baseline
panels.description: Takeout-only: the API sync has no breathing-rate puller, so this stops updating past the last backfill on a sync-only deployment.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT value AS "breaths/min", value - median AS "vs 30d median"
FROM health.daily_baseline WHERE metric = 'breathing_rate' ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1

panels.title: Skin temperature deviation vs 30d baseline
panels.description: An elevated deviation above the usual baseline can be an early illness signal. Takeout-only: the API sync has no temperature puller, so this stops updating past the last backfill on a sync-only deployment.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT value AS "skin temp (C)", value - median AS "vs 30d median"
FROM health.daily_baseline WHERE metric = 'nightly_temp' ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1

panels.title: Yesterday's heart rate
panels.description: The 24h leading up to last night's bedtime (primary_sleep_session.start_time), not a UTC/civil-day 'yesterday' - anchored to the actual sleep episode for the same drift-free reason as panel 2. X axis is hours before bedtime (negative = earlier).

targets.rawSql:
WITH ps AS (SELECT start_time FROM health.primary_sleep_session ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1)
SELECT
  EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (time_bucket('5 minutes', h.time) - ps.start_time)) / 3600.0 AS hours_before_bedtime,
  avg(h.bpm) AS bpm
FROM health.heart_rate h, ps
WHERE h.time >= ps.start_time - interval '24 hours' AND h.time < ps.start_time
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Yesterday's activity
panels.description: Still calendar-day 'yesterday' (steps_daily), not bedtime-anchored like the panel above - a stat tile isn't subject to the axis-drift problem, so it wasn't in scope for this fix. Worth reconciling later so both panels mean the same 'yesterday'.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  (SELECT steps FROM health.steps_daily WHERE day = (now() AT TIME ZONE '$timezone')::date - 1) AS "steps",
  (SELECT sum(minutes) FILTER (WHERE zone IN ('CARDIO', 'PEAK')) * 2 + sum(minutes) FILTER (WHERE zone = 'FAT_BURN')
     FROM health.azm_hourly
     WHERE hour >= (((now() AT TIME ZONE '$timezone')::date - 1)::timestamp AT TIME ZONE '$timezone')
       AND hour <  ((now() AT TIME ZONE '$timezone')::date::timestamp AT TIME ZONE '$timezone')) AS "AZM"

panels.title: Today's heart rate
panels.description: Since waking up (primary_sleep_session.end_time) - keeps growing through the day as new samples sync in, same live-updating idea as panel 13. X axis is hours since wake.

targets.rawSql:
WITH ps AS (SELECT end_time FROM health.primary_sleep_session ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1)
SELECT
  EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (time_bucket('5 minutes', h.time) - ps.end_time)) / 3600.0 AS hours_since_wake,
  avg(h.bpm) AS bpm
FROM health.heart_rate h, ps
WHERE h.time >= ps.end_time
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Today's activity so far
panels.description: Live cumulative steps/AZM since local midnight, from the real-time-enabled hourly aggregates — updates through the day, not just at report time.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  (SELECT sum(steps) FROM health.steps_hourly WHERE hour >= ((now() AT TIME ZONE '$timezone')::date::timestamp AT TIME ZONE '$timezone')) AS "steps today",
  (SELECT sum(minutes) FILTER (WHERE zone IN ('CARDIO', 'PEAK')) * 2 + sum(minutes) FILTER (WHERE zone = 'FAT_BURN')
     FROM health.azm_hourly
     WHERE hour >= ((now() AT TIME ZONE '$timezone')::date::timestamp AT TIME ZONE '$timezone')) AS "AZM today"

SELECT weight_kg AS "weight (kg)"
FROM health.weight
WHERE time >= ((now() AT TIME ZONE '$timezone')::date::timestamp AT TIME ZONE '$timezone')
ORDER BY time DESC LIMIT 1
